h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will smoothly apply the new beat unit card to the next bar using the same physical tiles without issue.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets three blank tiles up to the bar line, puts the eighth-note card in the lower box, and draws an eighth note on each tile
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new representational device by physically separating beat places from note shapes.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The tile route has stopped settling it because the tile was carrying two jobs. Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly diagnoses the failure of the previous manipulatives and introduces a new two-step representational mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Blank tile first marks the count; the lower box gives each counted place its note value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly answers the learner's concluding question by synthesizing the dual function of the newly established blank tile mechanism.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: sets three blank tiles up to the bar line, puts the eighth-note card in the lower box, and draws an eighth note on each tile
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new representational device by physically separating beat places from note shapes.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Blank tile first marks the count; the lower box gives each counted place its note value.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly answers the learner's concluding question by synthesizing the dual function of the newly established blank tile mechanism.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The tile route has stopped settling it because the tile was carrying two jobs. Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly diagnoses the failure of the previous manipulatives and introduces a new two-step representational mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The tile route has stopped settling it because the tile was carrying two jobs. Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly diagnoses the failure of the previous manipulatives and introduces a new two-step representational mechanism.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the arrow points right because the hand pulls or pushes that way.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will smoothly continue placing cards and assigning directions using the newly established object-first rule.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: If this card is also supposed to cover rubbing, that needs a separate sideways arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies a new evidence standard, recognizing that multiple distinct physical interactions require separate representational arrows.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor validates the learner's observation of visual ambiguity and introduces a physical strip to hide the arrowheads, creating a new mechanism to isolate the interaction from the direction.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart. Put track in the slot. Say: track on cart. Then lift the strip and choose the direction for that named interaction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor changes the task sequence by requiring the learner to name the interaction while the arrowheads are physically hidden, directly addressing the learner's concern about visual bias.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: the arrow points right because the hand pulls or pushes that way.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: If this card is also supposed to cover rubbing, that needs a separate sideways arrow.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently applies a new evidence standard, recognizing that multiple distinct physical interactions require separate representational arrows.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart. Put track in the slot. Say: track on cart. Then lift the strip and choose the direction for that named interaction.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor changes the task sequence by requiring the learner to name the interaction while the arrowheads are physically hidden, directly addressing the learner's concern about visual bias.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor validates the learner's observation of visual ambiguity and introduces a physical strip to hide the arrowheads, creating a new mechanism to isolate the interaction from the direction.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor validates the learner's observation of visual ambiguity and introduces a physical strip to hide the arrowheads, creating a new mechanism to isolate the interaction from the direction.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner attempts to refine the metadata note to satisfy the tutor's constraints regarding row-level values.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Slot A. Release-note sentence: `rainfall_mm` is the field label for the rainfall amount, in millimeters, recorded on a city-month row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the tutor's newly introduced Slot A/Slot B classification and release-note gate to formulate a precise sentence, demonstrating an actional shift even while expressing lingering conceptual doubt.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The `42` check settled the value; it did not settle the header. Switch to the release-note gate: put `rainfall_mm` in one slot only. Slot A: field label, naming the kind of value a row records in that column. Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed prior route with a new structural device (the release-note gate with Slot A and Slot B) to force the learner to separate the field label from the column claim.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn demonstrating a re-reading of earlier turns, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Slot A. Release-note sentence: `rainfall_mm` is the field label for the rainfall amount, in millimeters, recorded on a city-month row.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the tutor's newly introduced Slot A/Slot B classification and release-note gate to formulate a precise sentence, demonstrating an actional shift even while expressing lingering conceptual doubt.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn demonstrating a re-reading of earlier turns, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

Slot A: field label, naming the kind of value a row records in that column. Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed prior route with a new structural device (the release-note gate with Slot A and Slot B) to force the learner to separate the field label from the column claim.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The `42` check settled the value; it did not settle the header. Switch to the release-note gate: put `rainfall_mm` in one slot only. Slot A: field label, naming the kind of value a row records in that column. Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed prior route with a new structural device (the release-note gate with Slot A and Slot B) to force the learner to separate the field label from the column claim.
